Job Description
Who we are:
lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and
other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create
transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing,
connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on
stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every
community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier,
thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused
environment for our people.
About this team:
lululemon Engineering is dedicated to building secure, reliable, and performant products for
our guests and partners. We embrace the philosophies of Agile, DevOps, and SRE to
Refer/Apply
accelerate our development process and provide the most enjoyable, inclusive, and
supportive work environment possible. We believe our journey is more fun when it is
collaborative, as we focus on the future instead of the past. As an Engineer, you will work as
part of a global team supported by our business and architecture partners to help us
collaboratively develop and deliver industry leading technology solutions that drive
lululemon’s business goals.
Who You Are
As an Senior Engineer, you will bring a high level of technical knowledge as well as strong
mentoring abilities. You will be counted on as a leader in your technology space as you
contribute to all areas of development and operations (pre-production to production). You will
work closely with a Technology Manager, using your experience and knowledge to guide a
team of Engineers though their day-to-day process, and provide a central escalation point for
production concerns. You will be part of an Agile production release team and may perform
on-call support functions as needed
A Day in the Life :

12+ years of hands-on software engineering experience with strong computer science
fundamentals.
5+ years of experience in designing and building scalable, secure, cloud-native
microservices using Java, Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, WebFlux, and TDD.
Proven experience developing and operating high-volume, low latency distributed
systems with strong performance, resiliency, and observability.
Refer/Apply

High-ownership, self-starter mindset with the ability to independently drive architecture,
design, and delivery for critical systems.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills to influence technical decisions and
mentor engineering teams.

project management platforms.
Integrated AI/ML Experience:

and data-driven decisioning

APIs) into backend services

for code generation, testing, and productivity improvements

components

ai_interview_transcript.question: Can you explain the key differences between a monolithic architecture and microservices architecture, and share an example from your experience where breaking down a monolith into microservices proved beneficial?
ai_interview_transcript.answer: record Karenge ismein Jaise first answer Ko Main Yahan per bolunga uska record Rahega aur second question third question ke liye pest Karunga vah Logon Ko yah Dikhane ke liye ki yah paise bhi kam karta hai aur bolane se bhi kam karta hai

ai_interview_transcript.question: I'd love to hear your technical thoughts! Could you please explain the key differences between monolithic architecture and microservices architecture? Based on your resume, you have experience migrating legacy applications to microservices at Bytemark - I'm particularly interested in hearing about that experience.
ai_interview_transcript.answer:
Sure.

A monolithic architecture is where the entire application is built and deployed as a single unit, with all modules tightly coupled in one codebase. This makes it simpler to start with, but as the system grows, it becomes harder to scale, slower to deploy, and more difficult to maintain.

Microservices architecture breaks the system into smaller, independent services, where each service handles a specific business capability. These services can be developed, deployed, and scaled independently, and they communicate through APIs or messaging systems like Kafka.

In my experience, we had a monolithic system that handled multiple domains such as order processing, payments, and notifications. As traffic increased, we started facing issues with long deployment cycles and difficulty scaling specific parts of the system.

We gradually broke the monolith into microservices—for example, separating Order Service, Payment Service, and Notification Service. These services communicated via REST and Kafka events.

This helped us scale high-traffic services independently, reduce deployment risk, and improve system stability. It also allowed teams to work independently on different services, w

strengths: Clear explanation of both architectures
Concrete example from actual experience
Mentioned specific technologies (Kafka, REST)
Explained business benefits
Natural conversational tone
analysis.feedback: Strong answer demonstrating good understanding of architectural concepts with relevant experience

ai_interview_transcript.question: That's a great example of microservices migration! Since you mentioned using Kafka for communication between services, I'd like to dive deeper into messaging. Can you explain the difference between synchronous and asynchronous communication in microservices, and when you would choose one over the other? Also, what are some challenges you've faced with eventual consistency in distributed systems?
ai_interview_transcript.answer:
Sure.

In microservices, communication can be either synchronous or asynchronous.

Synchronous communication typically happens over REST APIs or HTTP, where one service directly calls another and waits for a response. It’s useful when an immediate response is required—for example, fetching user details or validating a request in real time. However, it introduces tight coupling and can increase latency, and failures in downstream services can directly impact the caller.

Asynchronous communication, on the other hand, uses messaging systems like Kafka. A service publishes an event and does not wait for a response. Other services consume the event independently. This approach improves decoupling, scalability, and fault tolerance, since services don’t depend on each other’s availability in real time.

In practice, I choose synchronous communication for real-time, request-response scenarios where the result is immediately needed, and asynchronous communication for event-driven workflows, background processing, and when loose coupling is important.

Regarding eventual consistency, one common challenge is data inconsistency across services because each service maintains its own database. For example, after an Orde

strengths: Clear distinction between synchronous and asynchronous communication
Appropriate examples for when to use each approach
Good understanding of trade-offs (tight coupling, latency, fault tolerance)
Started addressing eventual consistency challenges with concrete example
Answer flows naturally and conversationally
analysis.feedback: Strong understanding of microservices communication patterns with good practical reasoning for choosing between approaches.

ai_interview_transcript.question: Excellent explanation of synchronous vs asynchronous communication! I noticed your resume mentions significant experience with database optimization - you achieved a 40% reduction in API response times. Can you walk me through a specific performance optimization scenario you've worked on? What was the bottleneck, how did you identify it, and what techniques did you use to resolve it?
ai_interview_transcript.answer:
Sure.

In one of the services at Bytemark, we noticed that a few critical APIs—especially related to order retrieval—were consistently showing high response times.

To identify the bottleneck, we first analyzed application logs and APM metrics. We noticed that most of the latency was coming from database calls rather than application logic. We then enabled SQL query logging and found a few heavy queries that were taking significant time to execute.

The main issue was with a query that was joining multiple large tables and also performing filtering on non-indexed columns. This was causing full table scans and high execution time.

To fix this, we took a few steps:

* Added proper indexes on frequently used filter and join columns
* Optimized the query by reducing unnecessary joins and selecting only required columns instead of using select *
* Introduced pagination to limit the amount of data being fetched
* In some cases, we denormalized a small part of the data to avoid repeated joins for frequently accessed fields

We also reviewed Hibernate/JPA usage and removed a few N+1 query issues by

strengths: Systematic approach to identifying bottlenecks using APM metrics and SQL logging
Specific technical solutions like proper indexing and query optimization
Mentions concrete techniques like pagination and denormalization
Shows understanding of N+1 query problems
Practical problem-solving methodology
analysis.feedback: Good systematic approach to performance optimization with concrete technical solutions. Shows practical experience with database tuning.

overall_feedback.overall_summary: Gul Mohammed demonstrated solid technical knowledge in microservices and backend development concepts, with authentic responses showing genuine experience. However, the interview started poorly with a complete failure on the first question (answered in a different language), which raises concerns about communication readiness. His subsequent answers showed good depth and practical understanding, but the initial response creates uncertainty about consistency and interview preparedness.
strengths: Strong understanding of microservices architecture with concrete examples from actual experience
Good grasp of distributed systems concepts including synchronous vs asynchronous communication patterns
Systematic approach to performance optimization with specific technical solutions
Authentic responses demonstrating genuine hands-on experience rather than theoretical knowledge
Mentioned relevant technologies that align well with the job requirements (Kafka, REST APIs, database optimization)
areas_for_improvement: Communication consistency - complete failure to respond appropriately to the first technical question
Interview preparedness and professional communication standards
Need to demonstrate more advanced architectural decision-making capabilities
Could benefit from showing broader system design thinking beyond individual component optimization
overall_feedback.recommendation_reason: While the candidate showed strong technical knowledge in 3 out of 4 responses with authentic, detailed answers, the complete failure on the first question (responding in a different language) raises significant concerns about communication readiness and interview professionalism. The 5.75/10 average score falls in the borderline range requiring human judgment.

components

full-time • 8-12 years • Bengaluru
About lululemon:
lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other
athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create
transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting,
and being well. We owe our success to our innovative products, commitment to our people, and
the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on
creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an
equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. As we continue to elevate

RefReer fae rC/Aanpdpildyate
capabilities in Product Systems, Merchandising and Planning, Digital Presence, distribution and
logistics, and corporate systems. Our team in India works as an extension of the global team on
projects of strategic importance.
Location Bengaluru
Employment Type full-time
Experience Range 8-12 years
Java Spring Boot AWS CICD
Must-have Skills
CI/CD Microservices
Job Description
Who we are:
lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and
other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create
transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing,
connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on
stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every
community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier,
thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused
environment for our people.
About this team:
lululemon Engineering is dedicated to building secure, reliable, and performant products for
our guests and partners. We embrace the philosophies of Agile, DevOps, and SRE to
Refer/Apply
accelerate our development process and provide the most enjoyable, inclusive, and
supportive work environment possible. We believe our journey is more fun when it is
collaborative, as we focus on the future instead of the past. As an Engineer, you will work as
part of a global team supported by our business and architecture partners to help us
collaboratively develop and deliver industry leading technology solutions that drive
lululemon’s business goals.
Who You Are
As an Senior Engineer, you will bring a high level of technical knowledge as well as strong
mentoring abilities. You will be counted on as a leader in your technology space as you
contribute to all areas of development and operations (pre-production to production). You will
work closely with a Technology Manager, using your experience and knowledge to guide a
team of Engineers though their day-to-day process, and provide a central escalation point for
production concerns. You will be part of an Agile production release team and may perform
on-call support functions as needed
A Day in the Life :
Must Haves:
Qualifications:
Are a main contributor in Agile ceremonies
Provide mentorship and facilitate engineering training for a team of Engineers
Perform and delegate engineering assignments to ensure production readiness is
maintained
Conduct research to aid in product troubleshooting and optimization efforts
Conduct research to guide product development and tools selection
Provide an escalation point and participate in on-call support rotations
Actively monitor key metrics and report on trends
Participate in our Engineering Community of Practice
Contribute to engineering automation, management or development of production level
systems
Contribute to project engineering design and standards verification
Perform reliability monitoring and support as needed to ensure products meet guest
expectations
Acknowledges the presence of choice in every moment and takes personal responsibility
for their life.
Possesses an entrepreneurial spirit and continuously innovates to achieve great results.
Communicates with honesty and kindness and creates space for others to do the same.
Leads with courage, knowing the possibility of greatness is bigger than the fear of failure.
Fosters connection by putting people first and building trusting relationships.
Integrates fun and joy as a way of being and working, aka doesn’t take themselves too
seriously.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science/engineering or equivalent
12+ years of hands-on software engineering experience with strong computer science
fundamentals.
5+ years of experience in designing and building scalable, secure, cloud-native
microservices using Java, Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, WebFlux, and TDD.
Proven experience developing and operating high-volume, low latency distributed
systems with strong performance, resiliency, and observability.
Refer/Apply
Advanced proficiency with event-driven and streaming platforms, particularly Confluent
Kafka (schemas, partitions, consumer groups, optimizations).
Strong cloud engineering expertise across AWS services (Lambda, ECS/EKS, SQS/SNS,
DynamoDB, API Gateway, IAM, networking).
Deep DevOps and automation experience with CI/CD, GitHub Actions/Jenkins,
Containerization (Docker), Kubernetes, Terraform/Helm, and GitOps workflows.
Strong analytical and diagnostic skills for troubleshooting complex production issues using
distributed tracing, JVM tuning, and log/metric insights.
Experience delivering software across the full SDLC, including Agile, DevOps, testing
automation, observability, and SRE practices.
High-ownership, self-starter mindset with the ability to independently drive architecture,
design, and delivery for critical systems.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills to influence technical decisions and
mentor engineering teams.
Familiar with modern engineering tools: GitHub, IntelliJ/Eclipse, Jira, Confluence, and
project management platforms.
Integrated AI/ML Experience:
Basic understanding of AI/ML concepts such as predictive analytics, anomaly detection,
and data-driven decisioning
Hands-on exposure integrating AI-powered services/APIs (e.g., AWS AI services, OpenAI
APIs) into backend services
Experience using AI-assisted development tools (GitHub Copilot, Amazon CodeWhisperer)
for code generation, testing, and productivity improvements
Familiarity with vector databases, embeddings, or LLM-based retrieval patterns (RAG)
Ability to evaluate AI-driven enhancements for OMS workflows (fraud detection, exception
prediction, inventory intelligence)
Understanding of ethical AI usage, data privacy, and secure integration practices for AI
components
Refer/Apply

DTL
full-time 8-12 years Bengaluru
About Role
Senior Engineer II - Java - DTL
full-time • 8-12 years • Bengaluru
About lululemon:
lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other
athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create
transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting,
and being well. We owe our success to our innovative products, commitment to our people, and
the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on
creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an
equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. As we continue to elevate
our shopping experience, our India Tech Hub plays a key role in expanding our technology
RefReer fae rC/Aanpdpildyate
capabilities in Product Systems, Merchandising and Planning, Digital Presence, distribution and
logistics, and corporate systems. Our team in India works as an extension of the global team on
projects of strategic importance.
Location Bengaluru
Employment Type full-time
Experience Range 8-12 years
Java Spring Boot AWS CICD
Must-have Skills
CI/CD Microservices
Job Description
Who we are:
lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and
other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create
transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing,
connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on
stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every
community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier,
thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused
environment for our people.
About this team:
lululemon Engineering is dedicated to building secure, reliable, and performant products for
our guests and partners. We embrace the philosophies of Agile, DevOps, and SRE to
Refer/Apply
accelerate our development process and provide the most enjoyable, inclusive, and
supportive work environment possible. We believe our journey is more fun when it is
collaborative, as we focus on the future instead of the past. As an Engineer, you will work as
part of a global team supported by our business and architecture partners to help us
collaboratively develop and deliver industry leading technology solutions that drive
lululemon’s business goals.
Who You Are
As an Senior Engineer, you will bring a high level of technical knowledge as well as strong
mentoring abilities. You will be counted on as a leader in your technology space as you
contribute to all areas of development and operations (pre-production to production). You will
work closely with a Technology Manager, using your experience and knowledge to guide a
team of Engineers though their day-to-day process, and provide a central escalation point for
production concerns. You will be part of an Agile production release team and may perform
on-call support functions as needed
A Day in the Life :
Must Haves:
Qualifications:
Are a main contributor in Agile ceremonies
Provide mentorship and facilitate engineering training for a team of Engineers
Perform and delegate engineering assignments to ensure production readiness is
maintained
Conduct research to aid in product troubleshooting and optimization efforts

systems

expectations
Acknowledges the presence of choice in every moment and takes personal responsibility
for their life.
Possesses an entrepreneurial spirit and continuously innovates to achieve great results.
Communicates with honesty and kindness and creates space for others to do the same.
Leads with courage, knowing the possibility of greatness is bigger than the fear of failure.
Fosters connection by putting people first and building trusting relationships.
Integrates fun and joy as a way of being and working, aka doesn’t take themselves too
seriously.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science/engineering or equivalent
12+ years of hands-on software engineering experience with strong computer science
fundamentals.
5+ years of experience in designing and building scalable, secure, cloud-native
microservices using Java, Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, WebFlux, and TDD.
Proven experience developing and operating high-volume, low latency distributed
systems with strong performance, resiliency, and observability.
Refer/Apply
Advanced proficiency with event-driven and streaming platforms, particularly Confluent
Kafka (schemas, partitions, consumer groups, optimizations).

DynamoDB, API Gateway, IAM, networking).

Experience using AI-assisted development tools (GitHub Copilot, Amazon CodeWhisperer)
for code generation, testing, and productivity improvements

components
